headline: East Malaysian Students Face 34% Higher Living Costs in Peninsular Universities
context: A 2025 student welfare survey found Sabah and Sarawak students studying in Peninsular Malaysia face 34% higher living costs due to travel, cultural adjustment, and food expenses.

cards.big: East Malaysian students pay 34% more to study in Peninsular Malaysia.
cards.sub: Return flights alone cost RM1,200-2,400 per trip. Most students can afford only 2 trips per year.

cards.big: 67% of Sabah and Sarawak public university students study in the Peninsula.
cards.sub: Limited public university capacity in East Malaysia forces cross-country migration.

cards.big: No cost-of-living allowance supplements JPA or MARA scholarships for East Malaysians.
cards.sub: All scholarship amounts are standardised nationally regardless of travel distance or displacement cost.

cards.big: Drop-out rates among East Malaysian students in Peninsular universities are 18%.
cards.sub: The national average is 9%. Financial stress and cultural isolation are primary factors.

cards.big: Treating Peninsular and East Malaysia as one cost zone when a flight home costs more than a month's food. The scholarship was designed for one half of the country.

Distance-adjusted scholarships and expanded East Malaysian university capacity would address a disparity that affects tens of thousands. The MA63 promise of equal partnership requires equal educational access.
